steps.title: Identify the starting value and growth
steps.content: The problem gives the first point directly: when $x=0$, $y=90$. A $20\%$ increase means the next value is $120\%$ of the old value, so each step multiplies by $1.20$.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $y=a(b)^x$. The value at $x=0$ is $a$, so $a=90$. A $20\%$ increase means the growth factor is $1.20$, so the model is $y=90(1.20)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The matching choice has the starting value $90$ and the growth factor $1.20$. The other choices either use the wrong starting value or the wrong multiplier, so A is correct.
